title: ginkgo: install on demand via `go get -u`
body:
Install ginkgo on demand via `go get -u` rather than keeping a copy of its
entire source code in the vendor dirctory.  The main motivation for that
is to make `golangci-lint` happy which is continuously throwing up on
the import of a program (i.e., ginkgo).  The linter is broken and stupid
as it ignores flags to ignore dirs and ignores build tags (at least some
linters do) which is blocking us from updating to newer versions.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

One more stab for updating the linter.

comments.body:
/hold
Let's not merge this until we've branched for 3.0

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [saschagrunert,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Why were we vendoring this, anyways?

LGTM, but CI is angry.

> Why were we vendoring this, anyways?

We wanted to ship CI dependencies in `./vendor` but golangci-lint really doesn't like importing a program (i.e., main) and is completely ignoring `--skip-dir` and build tags (varying across versions and individual linters).  I couldn't see a way forward without getting that out of golangci-lint's way :(
Had to re-add `github.com/nxadm/tail v1.4.6 // indirect` to make CI happy
